title: Mammographic Breast Density Classification BI-RADS (PWDR)
sub_domain: Mammographic glandular-tissue density reconstruction with BI-RADS density category readout
source_data_quality_note: v2 physical_with_discrete_readout (PWDR) genesis Principle. Wraps L1-036 mammography (analytical core) with the deterministic BI-RADS Atlas (5th edition) density-category rule on volumetric glandular-tissue fraction. Universal clinical use — every screening mammogram in the United States since 2014 is graded with a BI-RADS density category by federal mammography quality standards (MQSA). UTSW Simmons Cancer Center runs high-volume breast-imaging; aligns with standard-of-care.

gate_substitutions.conservation_substitute: softmax simplex closure on BI-RADS density category {A=almost_entirely_fatty, B=scattered_fibroglandular, C=heterogeneously_dense, D=extremely_dense} ordinal readout

discrete_readout.threshold_function: BI-RADS_density(breast) = category_k such that volumetric_glandular_fraction(breast) in [tau_k, tau_{k+1}); tau_thresholds per ACR BI-RADS Atlas 5th edition (2013): A: <25%, B: 25-50%, C: 50-75%, D: >75%; secondary thresholds incorporate spatial-distribution heterogeneity (heterogeneously dense vs homogeneously dense) per Volpara/Quantra automated software validation.
discrete_readout.underlying_physical_state: volumetric breast composition map: glandular-tissue fraction g(r) per voxel and integrated VBD = integral g(r) dV / volume_breast, recovered from full-field digital mammography (FFDM) or digital breast tomosynthesis (DBT) projections via L1-036 mammography analytical core
discrete_readout.threshold_continuity_proof: Volumetric breast density is a continuous-valued integrated functional of the recovered glandular-fraction map g(r). BI-RADS category boundaries at 25%/50%/75% are step functions; smoothed-grading is globally Lipschitz with constant ~0.04 per percentage-point of VBD. Inherits stability from L1-036 mammography reconstruction.

related_principles: L1-036 (mammography — full-field digital mammography analytical core, physics_core_principle)
L1-063 (DBT — digital breast tomosynthesis sibling, informational)

E.description: Mammographic Breast Density Classification (PWDR): wraps L1-036 mammography analytical core with the ACR BI-RADS Atlas 5th edition density-grading rule. Stage 1 (analytical, from L1-036): reconstruct breast tissue composition g(r) (glandular-tissue volume fraction per voxel) from FFDM cranio-caudal + medio-lateral-oblique views via dual-energy decomposition or single-energy calibration with reference to fat/fibroglandular calibration phantoms. Compute volumetric breast density VBD = integral g(r) dV / volume_breast. Stage 2 (deterministic threshold): apply BI-RADS thresholds at 25%/50%/75% to assign category {A, B, C, D}; secondary spatial-heterogeneity rule discriminates heterogeneously vs homogeneously dense in C and D categories. Difficulty tier delta = 3 inherited from L1-036. Mismatch parameters: compression_thickness_calibration_error, exposure_factor_drift, calibration_phantom_uncertainty, age_related_density_shift, automated_vs_radiologist_disagreement.
E.forward_model: Stage 1 (L1-036): {y_projection_CC, y_projection_MLO} -> g(r) volumetric glandular fraction. Stage 2 (BI-RADS): VBD = integral g(r) dV; category = step(VBD at [25%, 50%, 75%]) per BI-RADS Atlas 5th edition.
E.world_state_x: Per-breast BI-RADS density category in {A, B, C, D}; optionally continuous-valued VBD for surveillance follow-up.
E.observation_y: Standard 4-view screening mammogram (left + right cranio-caudal + medio-lateral-oblique projections); typically 3000x4000 pixel FFDM detector at 70-100 micrometer pixel pitch.

G.dag: L.xray_source -> L.attenuation_projection -> L.dual_energy_decomposition -> L.glandular_fraction_recovery -> L.vbd_aggregation -> L.birads_threshold_classifier -> int.spatial
coupling_constraints: fraction_to_vbd: per-voxel glandular fraction integrates over breast volume to yield single VBD scalar; coupling between local tissue composition and aggregated breast-level density

W.regime: Existence and uniqueness inherited from L1-036 mammography. Stability inherits L1-036's well-conditioned reconstruction plus a small additive contribution from compression_thickness_calibration_error (dominant systematic bias source) and age_related_density_shift. Joint Hadamard well-posedness for the coupled mammography + BI-RADS threshold forward established by Sickles E et al. (2013 BI-RADS Atlas 5th ed), Highnam R et al. (2010 Volpara volumetric density), Hartman K et al. (2013 Quantra), Yaffe MJ (2008 review of mammographic density), Boyd NF et al. (2007 density and breast cancer risk), and Pertuz S et al. (2014 automated density estimation comparison).

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [L1-036 reconstruction] + categorical-readout [BI-RADS threshold] | end-to-end deep neural [DenseNet, ResNet50-Density] with explicit physics-informed glandular-fraction regularization
C.error_bound: Inherits L1-036 reconstruction bound; classification stage adds C_thresh * |VBD_error| / d(VBD, tau_boundary). Achieves q=2 convergence on reconstruction; classification stage inherits with additive calibration floor.
C.complexity: O(H_proj * W_proj * N_views) for stage 1 reconstruction (inherited); O(N_voxels) for VBD aggregation + O(1) for BI-RADS threshold; total stage-1-dominated

recast_attempted.recast_path: Original framing: 'Classify mammograms into BI-RADS density categories' (would be data_driven_statistical CNN). Option-3 recast: 'Reconstruct volumetric glandular-tissue fraction g(r) via dual-energy mammography decomposition; compute VBD; apply BI-RADS Atlas 5th edition thresholds at 25%/50%/75%'. Feasible because breast composition has a well-defined dual-energy x-ray forward model and BI-RADS categories are deterministic functions of recovered VBD.

joint_well_posedness_references: Sickles EA, D'Orsi CJ, Bassett LW et al. (2013) ACR BI-RADS Mammography. ACR BI-RADS Atlas, Breast Imaging Reporting and Data System, 5th ed.
Highnam R, Brady M, Yaffe MJ, Karssemeijer N, Harvey J (2010) Robust breast composition measurement — VolparaDensity. IWDM 2010, LNCS 6136:342-349.
Hartman K, Highnam R, Warren R, Jackson V (2013) Volumetric assessment of breast tissue composition from FFDM images: feasibility of a model-based approach. IWDM 2008.
Yaffe MJ (2008) Mammographic density. Measurement of mammographic density. Breast Cancer Res 10(3):209.
Boyd NF, Guo H, Martin LJ et al. (2007) Mammographic density and the risk and detection of breast cancer. NEJM 356(3):227-236.
Pertuz S, McDonald ES, Weinstein SP, Conant EF, Kontos D (2014) Fully automated quantitative estimation of volumetric breast density from digital breast tomosynthesis images. Radiology 279(1):65-74.
Wanders JOP et al. (2017) Volumetric breast density and breast cancer risk: comparison of two automated methods. Breast Cancer Res 19:67.

distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-036 mammography recovers projection / volumetric tissue contrast; L1-515 wraps with BI-RADS density-grading rule. Different solution_space (1D BI-RADS density category vs 2D mammographic projection); adds L.dual_energy_decomposition, L.glandular_fraction_recovery, L.vbd_aggregation, L.birads_threshold_classifier primitives.

v3_metadata.clinical_context: Universal clinical use — every screening mammogram in the United States since 2014 is graded with a BI-RADS density category, mandated by federal Mammography Quality Standards Act (MQSA). Density category influences supplemental screening decisions: women with category C or D ('dense breasts') have higher breast cancer risk and reduced mammography sensitivity, often qualifying for supplemental screening US or MR. UTSW Simmons Cancer Center runs the largest breast-imaging program in North Texas; aligns directly with FDA-mandated standard-of-care. Commercial automated systems: Volpara, Quantra (FDA-cleared); deep-learning variants (DenseNet density classifier) also FDA-cleared. PWDR formulation provides physics-grounded explainability: density category is derived from explicit volumetric glandular fraction, not black-box CNN.
